---
title: "The ACQUIRE checklist"
subtitle: "Verifiable statements about how a study was conducted."
toc: true
---

Clinical trials have CONSORT. Systematic reviews have PRISMA. Observational
studies have STROBE. In-the-wild sensing has nothing comparable — no agreed
statement of what a study should have done and reported for its measurements to
be assessable by a reader.

This checklist is a first attempt. It is generated from
[`checklist/acquire-checklist.yml`](https://github.com/acquire-framework/acquire-framework.github.io/blob/main/checklist/acquire-checklist.yml),
which is the single source of truth for the site, the package, and the exported
artifact.

## How to use it

Work through the items relevant to your study type, then attach the completed
checklist as supplementary material alongside your paper or dataset.

**Items marked not applicable require a written justification.** Silent omission
and reasoned exclusion look identical in a finished document, and only one of
them is honest. Requiring the justification is what makes the completed artifact
worth a reader's trust.

```{python}
#| echo: false
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Markdown

spec = yaml.safe_load(Path("checklist/acquire-checklist.yml").read_text())

types = spec["study_types"]
lines = [f"**Version {spec['version']}** · updated {spec['updated']} · "
         f"{len(spec['items'])} items\n"]
lines.append("\n**Study types used for filtering:**\n")
for key, desc in types.items():
    lines.append(f"- `{key}` — {desc}")
Markdown("\n".join(lines))
```

## Items

```{python}
#| echo: false
from IPython.display import Markdown

STAGE_ORDER = ["design", "instrument", "pilot", "deploy", "monitor", "curate", "release"]
by_stage = {}
for item in spec["items"]:
    by_stage.setdefault(item["stage"], []).append(item)

out = []
for stage in STAGE_ORDER:
    items = by_stage.get(stage, [])
    if not items:
        continue
    out.append(f"### {stage.capitalize()}\n")
    out.append(f"[{len(items)} items]{{.acq-label}}\n")
    out.append("| | Statement | Applies to | Recipe |")
    out.append("|:--|:--|:--|:--|")
    for item in items:
        applies = ", ".join(f"`{t}`" for t in item.get("applies_when", []))
        recipe = item.get("recipe")
        link = f"[detail](../{recipe}.qmd)" if recipe else ""
        out.append(
            f"| `{item['id']}` | {item['statement'].strip()} | {applies} | {link} |"
        )
    out.append("")
Markdown("\n".join(out))
```

## Export

Machine-readable export is planned for v0.2: a completed checklist will be
emitted as YAML and as a formatted PDF, each stamped with the checklist version,
the date, and the DOI of the release that produced it, so a reader can tell
exactly which version of the standard a study was assessed against.

For now, the source YAML can be copied and completed directly.

## This is a draft standard

Version 0.1.0 is one group's opinion, not a community consensus, and should be
read that way. It becomes a standard only if other groups use it, disagree with
it in public, and shape it. Disagreement about specific items — especially items
you think are wrong, unmeasurable, or missing — is the most useful contribution
at this stage.
